states.name: Cyrus
states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Devil. Fifteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of duplicity, corruption, and inescapable fate.

Cyrus learned to read the cards as a boy, the poor bastard of a lord. Even as a child, he was no fool; his use to his father would end the moment his wife bore him a true heir. Rather than wait to be unceremoniously murdered, he ran away and joined an underground ring of thieves masquerading as soothsayers and fortune tellers. His skill with a deck served him well, but unlike his comrades, Cyrus told no lies.

The misfortunes he predicted had a way of coming true.

states.name: Cyrus
states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Tower. Sixteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of suffering, disaster, and unforeseen consequences.

It was a simple enough job. A mercenary named Rody hired him to investigate a collapsing mine. Nothing more than a tidy purse of gold should have come of it. But somehow, Cyrus found himself embroiled in a fight to save the world.

The others at Rody's side were good, honest. Not like him. They played their parts in the grand pantomime well, but he could see the Pandemonium for what it was. A scheme.

Somewhere, someone was pulling their strings, and when everything finally went wrong, he vowed to find out who.

states.name: Cyrus
states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Star. Seventeenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of clarity, hope, and renewed purpose.

After much searching, he found his answers. Their god was the greatest con man of all -- but he did not run his game without reason. In the divine darkness, Cyrus glimpsed the worlds beyond Erden, and all the unknowable powers that resided there. He understood then that Rody had died not to protect Erden from the Pandemonium, but to protect it from them.

He would not let his sacrifice go to waste. As his friend had before him, he would take on the mantle of a god and play a newfound role.
